name: convolutional-handwriting-gan
description: ScrabbleGAN: Semi-Supervised Varying Length Handwritten Text Generation (CVPR20)
readme_summary: ScrabbleGAN is a PyTorch implementation for semi-supervised handwritten text generation. It focuses on generating realistic varying-length handwritten text, leveraging both labeled and unlabeled data for improved performance. The project includes scripts for supervised and semi-supervised training, dependency management, and data preparation.
key_features: Data preparation scripts for common datasets (IAM, RIMES, CVL)
Visdom integration for visualization during training
primary_use_case: Generating realistic, variable-length handwritten text using semi-supervised learning techniques.
cover_image_prompt: A master calligrapher, depicted as a wise artisan, sits at a wooden desk in a sunlit studio. Before them, a blank parchment is being transformed into a flowing, realistic handwritten letter. On one side of the desk, a small stack of labeled 'training data' scrolls are visible, while on the other, a larger, more chaotic pile of unlabeled 'inspiration' documents are scattered. The calligrapher's pen glows with a subtle digital aura, connecting the ink to a small, transparent screen displaying the repository name 'ScrabbleGAN' and abstract data patterns. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with clear outlines, emphasizing the creative process of generating authentic handwriting from diverse data sources.

name: prism-desktop
description: A customizable desktop dashboard for Home Assistant.
readme_summary: Prism Desktop is a lightweight, customizable Home Assistant client for Windows and Linux, offering system tray integration and real-time control of smart home devices without needing a web browser. It leverages Home Assistant's WebSocket API to provide instant updates and allows users to manage lights, thermostats, cameras, and automations through a sleek, interactive dashboard.
primary_use_case: To provide a native desktop application for controlling and monitoring Home Assistant devices on Windows and Linux, offering a more integrated and accessible experience than a web browser.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized robot assistant perched on a minimalist desk, holding a glowing orb that displays a dynamic, real-time dashboard of a smart home (lights, thermostat, camera feeds). The robot is subtly interacting with the orb, perhaps adjusting a virtual dial. Around the desk, subtle digital particles and faint lines of code suggest a technological interface. The background is a clean, modern room, with a soft glow emanating from the orb. The overall mood is helpful and efficient. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, bright color palette and clear, friendly character design, emphasizing ease of use and direct control.

name: netviz
description: A browser-based app for designing network architectures visually.
readme_summary: Netviz is a browser-based application for visually designing network architectures. It allows users to add, connect, and customize network components like servers and databases, offering features for organization, custom icons, and local saving without a server.
primary_use_case: Visually designing and documenting network architectures in a web browser.
cover_image_prompt: A master architect standing in a bright, airy workshop, holding a blueprint that is magically assembling itself into a complex, interconnected digital network. Servers, routers, and data streams are depicted as glowing, stylized building blocks. The architect points to a specific section, and a small, interactive UI element appears, showing options to customize that block. Subtle lines of code and data flow around the scene, hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is creative and empowering, with a clean, modern aesthetic. 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant colors and clear, crisp details.

name: voicebox
description: The open-source AI voice studio. Clone, dictate, create.
readme_summary: Voicebox is an open-source, local-first AI voice studio that allows users to clone voices, generate speech in multiple languages and TTS engines, and use voice input for dictation. It provides a complete voice I/O stack running locally for enhanced privacy and control, offering an alternative to cloud-based services like ElevenLabs and WisprFlow.
primary_use_case: To provide users with a comprehensive, privacy-focused, and locally-run AI voice studio for voice cloning, speech generation, and voice input.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a cozy, personal workshop, meticulously sculpting a voice from raw sound waves. The artisan holds a microphone (input) in one hand, capturing a snippet of audio, and a chisel in the other, shaping a glowing, ethereal voice (output) that takes the form of a unique, personalized avatar. Around the workshop, shelves are filled with diverse audio samples and small screens display waveform visualizations and language options. The repository name 'Voicebox' is subtly etched onto a workbench. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing creativity and personal control over AI voice technology.

name: skills
description: Skills for Real Engineers. Straight from my .claude directory.
readme_summary: This repository provides a collection of shell-based 'skills' for real engineers, designed to enhance the capabilities of coding agents. These skills are intended to be small, adaptable, and composable, aiming to improve agent performance by addressing common failure modes like misalignment and verbosity.
key_features: Shell-based agent skills for coding agents
Addresses common agent failure modes (misalignment, verbosity)
Includes skills like `/grill-me` and `/grill-with-docs` for detailed alignment
Facilitates creation of a shared language document for agents
Quickstart installer via `npx skills@latest add mattpocock/skills`
Configurable issue tracker integration (GitHub, Linear, local files)
primary_use_case: To enhance the functionality and reliability of AI coding agents by providing a set of practical, adaptable skills that improve alignment, reduce verbosity, and facilitate better communication between developers and agents.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, seasoned engineer character is meticulously assembling a complex, intricate clockwork mechanism. Gears and cogs, representing different coding tasks, are being precisely fitted together. Input streams of raw data and abstract ideas are flowing into the mechanism, and the output is a perfectly functioning, elegant software application depicted as a glowing, finished product. Subtle digital particles and small, stylized UI elements are integrated into the scene, hinting at the AI agent's involvement. The overall mood is one of focused craftsmanship and intelligent design, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a balanced color palette.

name: ralph
description: Ralph is an autonomous AI agent loop that runs repeatedly until all PRD items are complete.
readme_summary: Ralph is an autonomous AI agent loop designed to repeatedly execute coding tasks until all Product Requirements Document (PRD) items are completed. It leverages AI coding tools like Amp or Claude Code, maintaining context through git history, progress files, and a structured PRD JSON format.
key_features: Autonomous AI agent loop for task completion
Supports Amp and Claude Code AI tools
Context persistence via git history, progress.txt, and prd.json
Iterative execution with fresh AI instances per loop
Workflow for PRD generation, conversion to JSON, and execution
Automatic branch creation, story implementation, and quality checks
primary_use_case: Automating the development process by having an AI agent iteratively complete tasks defined in a Product Requirements Document.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent robotic architect meticulously building a complex structure, piece by piece, from a blueprint that transforms into digital code. The architect consults a glowing scroll labeled 'PRD' and places digital bricks representing completed tasks. Small, subtle screens around the scene display lines of code and progress bars. The overall scene is clean and futuristic, with a focus on the iterative building process. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, optimistic color palette, emphasizing the organized and automated nature of software development.
